project_summary.purpose: Provide a shared reading-community platform where authenticated users create book reading sessions, join sessions, track chapter progress, discuss in a single thread, and react with likes.
project_summary.target_users: Book readers, reading clubs, and small communities coordinating progress around the same book across web and mobile clients.
project_summary.core_problem_solved: Coordinates collaborative reading progress and discussion in one place with access control, ownership flows, and real-time updates.
main_use_cases: Sign up/sign in with email and password
Create a reading session with title, author, chapter count, description, visibility, and join policy
Discover sessions via search, visibility filter, and active/archived views
Join open sessions or request access to request-based sessions
Approve/reject join requests as session owner
Submit chapter progress and view progress bars
Post comments in a single session thread
Update profile display name (web and mobile) and avatar (web upload)

architecture: Monorepo-style dual-client architecture with a React + Vite web app and a React Native + Expo mobile app sharing one Supabase backend. No custom application server layer is present; clients call Supabase Auth, Postgres tables, RPC functions, Realtime channels, and Storage directly. Domain types and feature flows are parallel across web and mobile, with client-side state orchestration in top-level App components and utility modules/hooks for derived session state.

tech_stack.frontend: Web: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + CSS. Mobile: React Native 0.76 + Expo 52 + TypeScript.
tech_stack.backend: Supabase managed backend (Auth, Postgres, Realtime, Storage, SQL functions/RPC, RLS policies).
tech_stack.database: PostgreSQL (Supabase) with pgcrypto extension and relational schema in supabase/schema.sql.
tech_stack.auth: Supabase Auth email/password sessions; mobile uses AsyncStorage-backed auth persistence.
tech_stack.deployment: Web intended for static Vite deployment; mobile via Expo workflow; backend hosted on Supabase. No CI/CD pipeline configuration is present in repository.

features: Bilingual UI support (English and Burmese) on web and mobile
Session creation via secure RPC (create_reading_session) with validation
Join policies: open join or request-based join
Session status lifecycle: active/archived with owner restore
Session discovery: title/author search + visibility filtering + active/archived tab
Progress updates by chapter number and session/member progress visualization
Like-only reactions per comment with toggle behavior
Realtime refresh subscriptions for comments, likes, progress updates, membership changes, and join requests
Profile display name update (web and mobile)
Avatar support: upload/preview/signed-url rendering on web; display only on mobile

data_models.name: profiles
relationships: Referenced by reading_sessions.creator_id, session_members.user_id, progress_updates.user_id, comments.user_id, comment_likes.user_id, session_join_requests.user_id
constraints: FK cascade on delete from auth.users

data_models.name: reading_sessions
fields: creator_id uuid FK -> profiles.id default auth.uid()

data_models.name: session_members
relationships: Membership bridge between users and sessions

data_models.name: progress_updates
relationships: Belongs to one session and one user

data_models.name: comments
relationships: Belongs to one session and one user

data_models.name: comment_likes
relationships: Belongs to one comment and one user

data_models.name: session_join_requests
fields: status text check in (pending, approved, rejected)
relationships: Belongs to one session and one user

operations: reading_sessions: select, update(status), insert via RPC result flow
session_join_requests: select, insert/upsert(request), update(approve/reject), delete(own request)
createSignedUrl for private path rendering fallback to getPublicUrl

state_management: Local component state with React hooks (useState/useEffect/useMemo/useCallback) in monolithic App-level containers (web and mobile). Derived state is centralized on web via useSessionDerivedState plus pure helpers in sessionData/sessionState. No Redux/Zustand/MobX; state refresh strategy relies on Promise.all data fetches and targeted realtime-triggered reloads.
routing_structure: No explicit route-based navigation in web or mobile. Web renders a single App shell (no react-router usage despite dependency). Mobile uses a single-screen ScrollView layout without navigation stack.
ui_components: Web: AuthView, AuthLoadingView, DashboardHeader, SessionListPanel, SessionDetailPanel, Avatar
Web layout blocks: profile card, session creation form, sessions list, detail pane (member progress + join requests + discussion)
Mobile: single App component with reusable ActionButton and section cards for auth, profile, session creation, session list, and session detail
Shared UI patterns: segmented toggles, pills/badges, progress bars, form fields, primary/secondary/ghost actions

colors: Mobile palette centered on #2a5bd7 primary with neutral blue-grays
design_system.typography: Web uses Google Fonts Outfit (body) and Space Grotesk (headings); mobile uses React Native system fonts with weight-based hierarchy.
design_system.spacing: Card and stack-based spacing system with compact gaps, rounded corners (10-20px), responsive breakpoints at 960px and 640px on web.
design_system.component_style: Soft, rounded, high-contrast cards with blue gradients, pill badges, segmented controls, outlined inputs, and animated button press/hover feedback.
design_system.theme: Light blue editorial/productivity style; no dark theme implementation.

coding_patterns: Feature orchestration in top-level App with colocated handlers
Pure transformation utilities for lookup maps and derived metadata
Defensive async error handling with early returns and per-operation busy flags
Parallel data loading with Promise.all for related datasets
Type-safe domain modeling through shared TypeScript interfaces
Small presentational component decomposition for web panels
folder_structure: Root web app at src with components/hooks/lib/i18n/types; mobile client isolated in mobile-app with parallel i18n/lib/types and App.tsx; Supabase SQL schema in supabase/schema.sql; static assets in public; config/tooling files (Vite, TypeScript, ESLint, Vitest) at repository root.
naming_conventions: TypeScript uses PascalCase for components/types, camelCase for variables/functions, and descriptive handler names (handleX). Database schema uses snake_case table/column names. UI state names use explicit suffixes (Loading, Busy, Draft, Error). Policy names in SQL are human-readable sentence strings.
dependencies: Web runtime: react, react-dom, @supabase/supabase-js, react-router-dom (installed but unused)
Web tooling: vite, typescript, eslint, vitest, @vitejs/plugin-react, @vitest/coverage-v8
Mobile runtime: expo, react-native, react-native-web, @supabase/supabase-js, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, react-native-url-polyfill, expo-status-bar
security_practices: Row Level Security enabled on all core tables
Authenticated-only read/write policy model across app data
Security definer helper function is_session_member to avoid recursive policy checks
Security definer RPC create_reading_session with explicit input validation and auth.uid enforcement
Restricted function grants (execute to authenticated, revoke from public)
Profile row creation trigger on auth.users insert plus backfill migration
Avatar upload type and size validation client-side before storage upload
performance_patterns: Memoized derived state (filtered sessions, selected session, like metadata, pending requests)
Lookup maps for memberships, progress, join statuses, and profile resolution
Batch data retrieval with Promise.all to reduce sequential latency
Scoped realtime channels per selected session to limit event scope
Selective detail refresh after mutations rather than full app reload in some paths
Progressive conditional queries (load likes only when comments exist)
reusable_logic: sessionState utilities: getPreferredSelectedSessionId, filterSessions, buildLatestChapterByUser, buildCommentMeta
avatar utilities: getInitials, file type/extension handling, signed/public URL resolvers
shared translation dictionaries and language typing
shared domain interfaces in web and mobile types files
limitations: No guest browsing; app requires authentication for all access
No owner moderation/delete flow for other users' comments
Mobile app does not implement avatar upload UI (display-name editing only)
No dedicated backend service layer for business logic beyond Supabase SQL/RPC
No explicit CI/CD, EAS pipeline, or deployment automation in repository
No offline-first sync/conflict handling; depends on network and realtime availability
No evidence of pagination/virtualization for large session/comment datasets
